title: Crop DNA Extraction with Lab-Made Magnetic Nanoparticles
author: Haichuan Wang

steps.title: Weigh FeCl2·4H2O (4
steps.instruction: Weigh FeCl2·4H2O (4.34g, 0.0218 mol).

steps.title: Weigh FeCl3·6H2O (10
steps.instruction: Weigh FeCl3·6H2O (10.84 g, 0.04 mol).

steps.title: Mix both FeCl2·4H2O and FeCl3·6H2O in...
steps.instruction: Mix both FeCl2·4H2O and FeCl3·6H2O in 94 ml deionized water well in a 500 ml glass flask. (the molar ratio of Fe(II) to Fe(III) is 0.55: 1).

steps.title: Adequately filter the solution with filter paper (Whatman Cat
steps.instruction: Adequately filter the solution with filter paper (Whatman Cat. 1440-005) to remove impurities.

steps.title: Add 50 ml NaOH (5
steps.instruction: Add 50 ml NaOH (5.0 M) into the solution to obtain black magnetic nanoparticles (pH = 14) in a 500 ml three-necked, round-bottomed flask provided with a magnetic stirrer.

steps.title: Heat at 60 °C for 30 minutes with vigorous stirring
steps.instruction: Heat at 60 °C for 30 minutes with vigorous stirring.

steps.title: Immediately slow stirring for 1 h...
steps.instruction: Immediately slow stirring for 1 h at 80 °C under a nitrogen atmosphere for a ferrofluid containing Fe3O4 nanoparticles.

steps.title: Add 5g (0
steps.instruction: Add 5g (0.08 mole) of SA powder into the suspension (~150 mL) of magnetic nanoparticles at a molar ratio of 0.55 Fe(II): 1 Fe(III): 2 SA.

steps.title: Stir consistently at 90°C for 4 h
steps.instruction: Stir consistently at 90°C for 4 h.

steps.title: Separate SA coated Fe3O4 from the...
steps.instruction: Separate SA coated Fe3O4 from the aqueous solution by letting round-bottomed flask sit on regular magnet for 30 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Remove aqueous solution by pouring
steps.instruction: Remove aqueous solution by pouring.

steps.title: Add 250 ml pure water into flask
steps.instruction: Add 250 ml pure water into flask.

steps.title: Stir on stirring station for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Stir on stirring station for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Let flask sit on regular magnet...
steps.instruction: Let flask sit on regular magnet for 30 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Repeat step 11-14 five times
steps.instruction: Repeat step 11-14 five times.

steps.title: Add 250 ml pure water
steps.instruction: Add 250 ml pure water.

steps.title: Measure pH with pH meter (Thermo...
steps.instruction: Measure pH with pH meter (Thermo Scientific Orion Star™ A215 Benchtop pH/Conductivity Meter), a neutral pH (pH 7) should be reached.

steps.title: If pH is not at 7,...
steps.instruction: If pH is not at 7, then repeat step 11-14 2-3 more times, until pH is at 7.

steps.title: Keep the final solution with SA...
steps.instruction: Keep the final solution with SA coated magnetic particles at room temperature in a sealed glass bottle (100 ml) for future application in DNA preparation.

steps.title: Collect leaf discs in ¼ inch...
steps.instruction: Collect leaf discs in ¼ inch from soybean at V2 stage, wheat at Feekes 2 stage and maize at V3 stage by using a paper puncher (Amazon, A7074005S).

steps.title: 5, 10 and 15 leaf discs...
steps.instruction: 5, 10 and 15 leaf discs per collection per crop per tube (1.1 ml bullet tubes), three reps per crop.

steps.title: Load the tubes on 2 ml Avan 96-well deep plate
steps.instruction: Load the tubes on 2 ml Avan 96-well deep plate.

steps.title: Add five 2 mm ceramic beads (Biospec
steps.instruction: Add five 2 mm ceramic beads (Biospec. Cat. 11079124zx) into each tube.

steps.title: Cover plate with cheese cloth and wrap with rubber band
steps.instruction: Cover plate with cheese cloth and wrap with rubber band.

steps.title: Turn on Virtis Freezemobile Lyophilizer (25x),...
steps.instruction: Turn on Virtis Freezemobile Lyophilizer (25x), temperature set at -80 °C and vacuum at 50 mT.

steps.title: Load the plate with tubes on...
steps.instruction: Load the plate with tubes on Lyophilizer (Virtis Freezemobile Lyophilizer (25x)) once the temperature and vacuum reach its setting.

steps.title: Lyophilize leaf discs on Lyophilizer overnight...
steps.instruction: Lyophilize leaf discs on Lyophilizer overnight at -80°C and 50 mT,

steps.title: Remove the plate and seal tube...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate and seal tube with strip of caps for 1.2 ml micro titer tubes and store at room temperature for DNA extraction.

steps.title: Collect 1, 2 and 3 dry...
steps.instruction: Collect 1, 2 and 3 dry wheat seed(s) and 1 dry soybean per 1.1 ml tube.

steps.title: Add eight 2 mm ceramic beads (Biospec
steps.instruction: Add eight 2 mm ceramic beads (Biospec. Cat. 11079124zx) into each tube and seal tube with cap.

steps.title: Add 500 µl extraction buffer into...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µl extraction buffer into each tube with seed(s) and leave tubes in 4 °C fridge overnight before grinded.

steps.title: Load the plate with tubes having...
steps.instruction: Load the plate with tubes having leave tissues on the TissueLyserII (Qiagen) by using TissueLyser Adapter Set.

steps.title: Shake for 30/s for 5 min
steps.instruction: Shake for 30/s for 5 min.

steps.title: Repeat step 24 one more time
steps.instruction: Repeat step 24 one more time.

steps.title: Remove tubes from tissuelyser
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from tissuelyser.

steps.title: Spin down at 3700 rpm for 30 sec
steps.instruction: Spin down at 3700 rpm for 30 sec.

steps.title: Add 500 µl of DNA extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µl of DNA extraction buffer (Tris-HCl 50 mM, pH 6.5, CTAB 1%, EDTA 10 mM, beta-mercaptoethanol 5%, 1.4 M NaCl and 2% PVP-40) into each tube.

steps.title: Seal tubes with strip of caps for 1
steps.instruction: Seal tubes with strip of caps for 1.2 ml tubes well

steps.title: Incubated plate with tubes having lyophlized...
steps.instruction: Incubated plate with tubes having lyophlized leaf tissues at 65°C for 60 min on a water bath.

steps.title: Clean the water on plate by using paper towel
steps.instruction: Clean the water on plate by using paper towel.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3700 rpm for 15 min at 25°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3700 rpm for 15 min at 25°C.

steps.title: For grinding the seeds in tubes,...
steps.instruction: For grinding the seeds in tubes, follow the step 32-36 and step 38-41.

steps.title: Take a new a new 0
steps.instruction: Take a new a new 0.2 ml 96-well non-skirt plate, add 2 µl beads (60 ng/µl) and 1 µl of RNase A (1 µg/µl) into each well bottom.

steps.title: Transfer 100 µl supernatant per tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µl supernatant per tube into well, one tube into one well.

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down 3 times and leave plate on bench for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 100 µl isopropanol into each well
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl isopropanol into each well.

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting up and down three times
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting up and down three times.

steps.title: Leave plate on bench for 10 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Leave plate on bench for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Let the plate sit on a...
steps.instruction: Let the plate sit on a magnet (dynaMag-96 side) for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove all solution carefully from each well by pipetting
steps.instruction: Remove all solution carefully from each well by pipetting. don't touch beads pellet on wall.

steps.title: Add 100 µl 80% EtOH without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl 80% EtOH without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Wait for 30 sec
steps.instruction: Wait for 30 sec.

steps.title: Remove EtOH by pipette and air dry for 5 min
steps.instruction: Remove EtOH by pipette and air dry for 5 min.

steps.title: Remove 96-well PCR plate from magnet
steps.instruction: Remove 96-well PCR plate from magnet.

steps.title: Add 50 µl 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8)
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8).

steps.title: Mix well with a pipette or slightly vortex
steps.instruction: Mix well with a pipette or slightly vortex.

steps.title: Spin down the plate
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate.

steps.title: Leave plate on bench for 10...
steps.instruction: Leave plate on bench for 10 min at room temperature (not on magnet).

steps.title: Place plate back on the magnet for 3-5 min
steps.instruction: Place plate back on the magnet for 3-5 min.

steps.title: Transfer solution (~ 50 µl) with...
steps.instruction: Transfer solution (~ 50 µl) with DNA into a new PCR plate.

steps.title: Leave the plate on ice
steps.instruction: Leave the plate on ice.

steps.title: Measure DNA concentration on Qubit (2
steps.instruction: Measure DNA concentration on Qubit (2.0) by following kit instructions (Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits, Q32850).

steps.title: Load 1 µl of DNA/sample/well on 1
steps.instruction: Load 1 µl of DNA/sample/well on 1.5% agarose gel with SYBR. Lambda DNA used as DNA marker.

steps.title: Label the plate, seal plate with...
steps.instruction: Label the plate, seal plate with foil plate seal (Thermo Scientific, Cat.232698) and store plate at -20°C freezer for future use.

steps.title: Run at 120 V for 45...
steps.instruction: Run at 120 V for 45 min in 1x TBE buffer.

steps.title: Evaluate the gel under UV Transilluminator...
steps.instruction: Evaluate the gel under UV Transilluminator (UVP) at 488 nm wavelength.

steps.title: Create a daughter plate to a...
steps.instruction: Create a daughter plate to a final concentration of 12 ng/µl/DNA sample for further evaluation by molecular inversion probe (MIP) and conventional PCR analysis, respectively.

steps.title: Store the plate with all DNA...
steps.instruction: Store the plate with all DNA samples in -20 °C freezer for use.

steps.title: Mix 5 µl of 2x master mix (BioRad, Cat
steps.instruction: Mix 5 µl of 2x master mix (BioRad, Cat. 172-5310), 0.25 µl of 10 µM primers (Forward and Revers primer respectively) and 1 µl genomic DNA in a 200 µl PCR tubes (Axygen, Cat. PCR02C) at a final reaction volume of 10 µl/sample.

steps.title: Conduct PCR with following conditions, 98...
steps.instruction: Conduct PCR with following conditions, 98 °C/30 s, 21x (98 °C/10 s, 60 °C/30 s, 72 °C/30 s), 72 °C/60 s, holding at 12 °C on BioRad C1000 thermal cycler.

steps.title: Load 3 µl of DNA/sample/well on 1
steps.instruction: Load 3 µl of DNA/sample/well on 1.5% agarose gel with SYBR along with 100 bp DNA ladder.

steps.title: Run at 120 V for 45...
steps.instruction: Run at 120 V for 45 min in 1x TBE buffer.

steps.title: Evaluate the gel under UV Transillumnator at 488 nm wavelength
steps.instruction: Evaluate the gel under UV Transillumnator at 488 nm wavelength.

steps.title: Dilute 998 individual probe synthesized by...
steps.instruction: Dilute 998 individual probe synthesized by Integrated DNA Technologies (Coralville, IA, USA) at 0.2 nmole (Supplemental Table S2, Wang et. al. 2022) with nuclease free water at 1:10.

steps.title: Pool 2 μl each probe together...
steps.instruction: Pool 2 μl each probe together in a 2 ml centrifuge tube.Phosphorylate the pooled probes in 200 μl PCR tube in a 20 μl reaction consists of 15 μl of diluted Pooled probes, 1× T4 DNA ligase buffer, 1 mM ATP, and 0.5 U of T4 polynucleotide kinase (ThermoFisher, T4 polynucleotide kinase kit).

steps.title: Close the cap and mix well
steps.instruction: Close the cap and mix well.

steps.title: Spin down
steps.instruction: Spin down.

steps.title: Load the PCR tube to thermal...
steps.instruction: Load the PCR tube to thermal cycler and incubate at 37 °C for 30 min followed by an inactivation of the kinase at 75 °C for 10 min.

steps.title: Spin down tube and leave tube...
steps.instruction: Spin down tube and leave tube on ice in an ice bucket. Make a master mix for hybridization and extension, which consists of 1.05 μl pooled phosphorylated

steps.title: probes (∼1,000:1 DNA to probe ratio), 1
steps.instruction: probes (∼1,000:1 DNA to probe ratio), 1.25 μl 10× Ampligase DNA ligase buffer (Epicentre Technologies), 1 μl of 5 M Betain (Sigma Aldrich), 3.25 U AmpliTaq DNA polymerase (ThermoFisher), 16 U of DNA Ampligase (Epicentre Technologies), 0.016 mM dNTP, and water. Note, this is for one reaction/well. Scale it up accordingly with your sample size.

steps.title: Take a new 384-well plate
steps.instruction: Take a new 384-well plate.

steps.title: Add 5
steps.instruction: Add 5.25 μl at 12 ng/ul (63 ng) DNA sample into corresponding well. One sample/well.

steps.title: Dispense the master mix from step...
steps.instruction: Dispense the master mix from step 8 into each well by using Mantis Liquid Handler (Formulatrix). The final reaction volume is 12.5 µl per reaction per sample per well.

steps.title: Seal the 384-well plate with film...
steps.instruction: Seal the 384-well plate with film by using PCR Plate Sealer and mix well by vortexing.

steps.title: Spin down
steps.instruction: Spin down.

steps.title: Leave plate on ice
steps.instruction: Leave plate on ice.

steps.title: Turn on the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Turn on the thermal cycler.

steps.title: Load the plate to thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Load the plate to thermal cycler.

steps.title: Run a program as following parameters
steps.instruction: Run a program as following parameters. 98 °C for 10 min at 60 °C for ∼24 h. Note, the heated lid for the PCR machine is set to 105 °C for the first 30 min and then changed to 80 °C after the first 30 min of the incubation.

steps.title: Stop PCR reaction and remove the plate
steps.instruction: Stop PCR reaction and remove the plate.

steps.title: Spin down plate, then leave the plate on ice
steps.instruction: Spin down plate, then leave the plate on ice.

steps.title: Remove the sealing film
steps.instruction: Remove the sealing film.

steps.title: Dispense 2 μl mix of 6
steps.instruction: Dispense 2 μl mix of 6.4 U of Exo I (New England Biolabs), 32 U of Exo III (New England Biolabs), 0.2 μl 1× Ampligase DNA ligase buffer into each well using a Mantis Liquid Handler (Formulatrix).

steps.title: Seal plate with seal film using...
steps.instruction: Seal plate with seal film using PCR Plate Sealer, mix plate well by vortexing and spin down.

steps.title: Load the plate to thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Load the plate to thermal cycler and incubate at 37 °C for 30 min followed by 95 °C for 2 min to digest uncircularized MIPs and DNA templates.

steps.title: Remove the plate from thermal cycler,...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from thermal cycler, spin down and leave it on ice.

steps.title: Make a PCR master mix 2
steps.instruction: Make a PCR master mix 2.5 μl of 2× iProof HF Master Mix (BioRad) and 0.75 μl water in a 200 μl PCR reaction tube. (Note, this volume is for one reaction per well, you need to scale up volume accordingly with your sample size).

steps.title: Close the cap mix well by vortexing
steps.instruction: Close the cap mix well by vortexing.

steps.title: Spin down and leave tube on ice
steps.instruction: Spin down and leave tube on ice.

steps.title: Take a new 384-well PCR plate
steps.instruction: Take a new 384-well PCR plate.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 μl of 10 μM indexed forward and reverse primers (Supplemental Table S2) per well.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.25 μl of circularized MIP capture taken from step 24 as template by using multichannel pipette.

steps.title: Dispense 3
steps.instruction: Dispense 3.75 μl master mix from step 27 into each well by using Mantis.

steps.title: Seal plate with seal film, vortex slightly to mix well
steps.instruction: Seal plate with seal film, vortex slightly to mix well.

steps.title: Spin down the plate
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate.

steps.title: Load the plate to the thermal cycler
steps.instruction: Load the plate to the thermal cycler.

steps.title: Run PCR by following conditions
steps.instruction: Run PCR by following conditions: 98 °C for 30 s; 21 cycles of 98 °C for 10 s, 60 °C for 30 s, 72 °C for 30 s;and 72 °C for 60 s, holding at 12 °C.

steps.title: Spin down the plate
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate.

steps.title: Remove the seal film
steps.instruction: Remove the seal film.

steps.title: Pool PCR reactions by taking 1 μl/well/sample in a 1
steps.instruction: Pool PCR reactions by taking 1 μl/well/sample in a 1.5 ml centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Mix well and spin down, then leave tube on ice
steps.instruction: Mix well and spin down, then leave tube on ice.

steps.title: Take 30 μl pooled PCR products...
steps.instruction: Take 30 μl pooled PCR products from step 39 and separate it on 1.5% agarose gel along with 50-bp (Fisher) and 100-bp DNA ladders (GoldBio) for 25-30 min at 120 V.

steps.title: Stain the gel with 1x SYBR...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel with 1x SYBR in running buffer for 5-10 min.

steps.title: Excise the sharp band at ∼286...
steps.instruction: Excise the sharp band at ∼286 bp by using clean razor blade.

steps.title: Purify gel from step 42 with...
steps.instruction: Purify gel from step 42 with gel extraction kit (Qiagen) by following the manufacturer's instructions.

steps.title: Quantify the purified PCR products on...
steps.instruction: Quantify the purified PCR products on Qubit (ThermoFisher) with Qubit DNA HS Assay Kit (ThermoFisher).

steps.title: Quantify purified pooled PCR products further...
steps.instruction: Quantify purified pooled PCR products further precisely by using the KAPA library quantification kit Illumina platforms (KAPA, KK4824) on a QuantStudio 6 Flex system (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: Sequence the purified PCR products on...
steps.instruction: Sequence the purified PCR products on NextSeq 550/500 system (Illumina) at 150 bp single read with customer specific sequencing primers (Supplementary Table S3).

steps.title: Filter the raw reads, including remove...
steps.instruction: Filter the raw reads, including remove adapter sequence contamination, truncated reads, and reads with overall low base quality the raw reads with Trimmomatic (software).

steps.title: Aligned the filtered reads to a...
steps.instruction: Aligned the filtered reads to a custom reference genome, which consists of 200-bp flanking regions around each targeted SNP for each of the 998 SNPs using Bowtie 2 in end-to-end mode with the sensitive setting.

steps.title: Discard reads with a mapping quality score of 20
steps.instruction: Discard reads with a mapping quality score of 20.

steps.title: Switch to MIP protocol
steps.instruction: Switch to MIP protocol
